description:
Recto: Legal notes, or maybe formularies and legal procedure. The top entry is a bill of divorce dated 1423 Seleucid, which is 1111/12 CE. Location: Fustat. There are two addenda, at least one of which certifies that the wife received the get. The document is quite damaged. 

Verso: Letter fragment in Judaeo-Arabic. Mentions the police (shurṭa), Maḥfūẓ, possibly Bū l-Surūr, the wālī of Cairo standing at the gate of [...], and 'the dīwān.' The hand is known. Very damaged. Needs examination.
description_he: מסמך משפטי בAramaic, Judaeo-Arabic מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
